blocks.content: The emperor's coronation of the Kashan Empire, the ruler of the northwestern continent and holder of one-third of the human realm's territory, was an hour away.
As always, the succession process was unofficial and secretive.
In the 23rd-floor spire of the grand palace Aganos, only the two Terajes, who shared the same memories, and a few who knew personal secrets were present.
The transfer of power from Teraje Mistra to Teraje Uorin.
To outsiders, it appeared to be a typical succession where a parent passed the throne to their child, but some knew the truth.
The only thing that changed was the name.
Teraje was the proper noun for 'something' that had lived through the ages, continuously renewing itself.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: With this, the handover related to the Holy War is complete. Well, if the Holy War is over, everything is done.
Fourteen hours ago, Uorin had visited the World Chamber in the central continent.
There, she was first introduced to the Holy War, and in an hour, she would ascend to the Triumvirate.
Even within the Holy War, the world's most powerful superpower, Teraje's influence was immense.
Below the Triumvirate were the Seven Kings, the rulers of the seven most powerful nations on the continent, and the two military leaders of the fairy and dragon races.
These twelve lords of the Holy War were the pinnacle of the world.
Uorin, who had become the ruler of the world from a princess of Kazra in an instant, showed no joy on her face.
After all, everything was just a subset of the name Teraje.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: No need for idle talk. We've already verified that there are no discrepancies between your memories and mine. But I have a question.
Uorin stopped and looked back at Mistra.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Why is this happening so quickly? I thought it would take another four years for me to ascend the throne.
Since the memories of the mother and daughter were shared, they were two yet one.
Therefore, any inflection point in their thoughts had to be addressed.

blocks.speaker: Mistra
blocks.content: Hmm, well...
Mistra looked back at Uorin.
While she had black hair and intense eyes, Uorin had white hair and a transparent impression.
Neither her husband, Orcamp, nor she had white hair.
Hence, a mutation.
She was 'herself,' possessing the rare 'future vision' among the abilities of 'history search' that made the current Teraje possible.

blocks.speaker: Mistra
blocks.content: Perhaps it was inevitable that Uorin would become the emperor among the many daughters.
However, today's coronation was an irregular move that even Mistra hadn't calculated.

blocks.speaker: Mistra
blocks.content: Don't worry. I'll ensure my disappearance. But there are memories that must be passed on since your birth, so I followed you to Aganos.
Once Uorin ascended to the Triumvirate, there was no reason for Mistra to exist in this world.
However, there was something she had to finish in her time.
Advancing the succession by several years and quickly pushing things forward by accusing Micaiah Gaold of treason were for that reason.
Entering the room, Mistra took a locked box from the desk drawer.
She handed it to Uorin, who was waiting on the sofa, and sat across from her, crossing her legs.

blocks.speaker: Mistra
blocks.content: This box contains all the answers to your questions. It's a secret of the current humanity that no one knows. If used well, it will be a powerful weapon, but given the situation, I had to act first.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: How do I open it? I can't break it.

blocks.speaker: Mistra
blocks.content: I have the key. I can't show it to you yet. Before that, there's someone I want to introduce to you.
Right on time, there was a knock on the door.
Only a handful of people in the world had the authority to enter the 23rd floor of the grand palace Aganos, so Mistra immediately told them to come in.
A tall man entered.
He had a kind impression with narrow eyes, his purple hair slicked back except for one strand.
It was Gando, the former head of security for the Tormia Magic Association.
As the first son of Teraje Mistra, he was biologically Uorin's brother, but psychologically, he was her son.
Bloodline meant nothing to Teraje.

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: You called, Mother.

blocks.speaker: Mistra
blocks.content: Yes, come here. You handled this well.
For Gando, who had been adopted into Tormia and raised by the Ganza, the Tormia Magic Association was his whole life.
But it was all a lie.
Even joining Gaold's inner circle was a false life designed by his mother.

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: Oh, why the face? Is it because of Gaold? You didn't have personal feelings, did you?

blocks.speaker: Mistra
blocks.content: The only person important to me is you, Mother. But... I respect his achievements as a magician.

blocks.speaker: Mistra
blocks.content: Sigh. Gando, you are Teraje's son. There's no need to respect a mere head of the Magic Association.
Gando remained silent.
The Triumvirate had the right to say such things.
But having spent most of his life in Tormia, he still couldn't tell which side was true.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: He's definitely different from Zion. This time, you chose well.

blocks.speaker: Mistra
blocks.content: Hehe, right? I didn't expect much from a son. Since he has talent, should we train him as a magician? You can use him if you want.
Gando lowered his head.

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: Chosen well? Train well? What was he to Teraje? A human? A creature? Or just a cog in the clockwork, meant to turn forever?
Uorin examined Gando and nodded.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Keep him? Hmm, I'm satisfied with Crouch. Well. Gando, come here.
Gando approached Uorin like a slave sold at the market.
Every time her gaze pierced him, he felt stripped naked.

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: He couldn't show his discomfort.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Well, let's keep him for now. I'll need to focus on Kashan for a while anyway.

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: Thank you, Mother.
Gando bowed deeply.
His eyes, looking at the floor, trembled.

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: He would die.
If Uorin was Mistra's incarnation, he couldn't trust any word from her mouth.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: But I have two conditions.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Speak. If it's something I can grant...

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: Ensure I don't conflict with anyone in Kashan.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Alright. The second?

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: Don't make me criticize you. I'm just a warrior fighting blindly for you.
It was a plea to be treated like a dog.

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: Desperate but unwilling to descend into abject humiliation.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: You've saved your life, Gando.

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: I am grateful for your mercy, Mother.
Mistra waved her hand.

blocks.speaker: Mistra
blocks.content: Leave now. There's still work to do.
This might be Gando's last meeting with Mistra, but there was no maternal warmth in her tone.
As Gando left, Mistra took out a key from her bosom, as if it was time.

blocks.speaker: Mistra
blocks.content: The reason for the accelerated succession is to create variables.

blocks.speaker: Mistra
blocks.content: Gaold will go to heaven and do something. Ultimately, it will benefit us.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Do you think he can succeed? A 20-year plan fell apart in an instant.

blocks.speaker: Mistra
blocks.content: Success doesn't matter. What matters is that something changes.

blocks.speaker: Mistra
blocks.content: Humans gamble on low odds because the reward of victory outweighs the despair of defeat. I just gave Gaold that chance.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: I still don't understand. I don't know what you're thinking. How can this be?

blocks.speaker: Mistra
blocks.content: Of course. That's why you need to open the box. It contains a deja vu of 'underlying events' that only I know.
Teraje held all memories from ancient times to the present and could quickly search through them with the unique ability 'history search.'
Initially, it was just simple data retrieval.
But with the Goffin Reset, this ability evolved explosively.
The history after the reset created 'underlying events' beneath the existing history.
Two different timelines sharing the same period created a form of future prediction through deja vu.

blocks.speaker: Mistra
blocks.content: With 'future vision' added, Uorin could potentially conquer heaven in her time.

blocks.speaker: Mistra
blocks.content: The reason I hid it until the end was to prevent subjective judgment from interfering with history search.

blocks.speaker: Mistra
blocks.content: You need to see and judge for yourself. After all, I am you, and you are me.
Mistra walked to the desk.
Her life's mission was complete once she handed over the key.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: How will you do it?

blocks.speaker: Mistra
blocks.content: Anything will do, but let's keep it clean.
Mistra picked up a dagger from the desk.
The blade, drawn with a swish, scattered the light in the room.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: I understand.
Uorin nodded and inserted the key into the box.
Meanwhile, Mistra gripped the handle with both hands, lifted her head, and placed the blade on her neck.
Blinking at the ceiling, she suddenly remembered and looked at Uorin.

blocks.speaker: Mistra
blocks.content: Right, you said you'd take Shirone. Is that still your plan?

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Ataraxia, and he's handsome, right? Nothing else seems important. Why, is there a problem?

blocks.speaker: Mistra
blocks.content: No. Alright then.
Mistra lifted her head again.
She had a slight bad feeling, but it was also a common occurrence.
The long history of Teraje was not weak enough to be affected by a mere boy.
As Uorin opened the box, Mistra's sword moved sharply, and a fountain of blood spurted out.
A moment later, there was a thud as Mistra's body fell.
Uorin didn't even glance at her.
Her interest was solely in the information inside the box.
The box contained documents on two matters.
The title on the cover of the first document was 'Goffin Erasure.'
Since the reset was estimated to have occurred 19 years ago, Uorin, born after that, had a hazier understanding of the underlying events than Mistra.
However, this was not particularly important information, as it had already been understood by the Holy War and major institutions of various countries.
Uorin set aside the first document, a relic of the old era, and took out the new document underneath.
The title on the cover of the new document was 'Anke La Erasure.'

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: This is it...
Her hand, which had been reaching for the coffee cup, paused and then returned to grasp the document with both hands.
She spent a long time savoring the title in the center of the document.
Anke La Erasure.
Uorin quickly flipped through the pages.
Storage first, analysis later.
In five minutes, she had memorized all 200 pages word for word.
She then brought a candlestick and burned the document.
Uorin's eyes, staring at the flames, were sharper than ever.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: You were right, Mistra.
Gando knocked and entered the room.
He hesitated for a moment at the completely changed scene but then returned to his calm expression and bowed.

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: Mother, it is time for the coronation.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: I understand. I will go now.
Gando turned to look at Mistra, who lay on the floor.
She had been beheaded with a sword.
Even without looking, he could tell it was a flawless cut.

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: What should we do with the body?

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Dispose of it.
Uorin's short answer indicated not to disturb her thoughts.
Gando carefully removed the body, making no noise with his footsteps.

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: She was his birth mother. But now, Mistra was Uorin. An existence beyond human common sense. That was Teraje.
Uorin strode out into the hallway.
At the end of the 23rd-floor tower was a balcony overlooking a square filled with hundreds of thousands of imperial citizens.
Drawing back the curtains and stepping onto the balcony, she saw the massive crowd stretching to the horizon.
A tremendous roar erupted from the ground upon seeing Teraje.
The earth shook, and the heat rose to the top.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Long live the Empress! Long live the Empress! Long live the Empress!
Uorin raised her hand.
The volume, which seemed unable to rise any higher, doubled, deafening her.
The whole world lay at her feet, but Uorin only looked down coldly.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Humans are unique creatures with as many personalities as there are individuals, but when gathered, they become a species bound by the single name of humanity.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Not someone, but the giant monster called humanity was roaring at her.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Shirone, there is no time. Meet Ikael. You must change things. If not...
Uorin looked up at the sky.
Only the sun was higher than her.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Humanity will perish.
